title: RPI: Resonant Permutation Inference - Zero-Multiply Language Generation via Cache-Resonant Permutation Routing
description: RPI is a language generation engine that eliminates all floating-point multiply-accumulate operations from inference. It replaces matrix multiplication with permutation table lookups and integer accumulation, achieving 18,000 tokens/second with model sizes of 0.3-3 MB. Introduces cache-resonance attention (using CPU cache hierarchy as implicit attention), NUMA-aware cell banking, and a dual-brain architecture for speculative LLM drafting. Runs on hardware from Nintendo 64 (1996) to IBM POWER8.
references: Vaswani et al. Attention Is All You Need. NeurIPS 2017.
Leviathan et al. Fast Inference from Transformers via Speculative Decoding. ICML 2023.
Boudreaux. RAM Coffers: NUMA-aware Neuromorphic Weight Banking. GitHub 2025.
